question: [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SableStakingV2.increaseF_SABLE() precision] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER observe that SABLEGainPerSABLEStaked = _SABLEGain.mul(DECIMAL_PRECISION).div(totalSableLPStaked) truncates when totalSableLPStaked is very large under PRECONDITIONS where totalSableLPStaked = 20935597236192701456120 (live value) and _SABLEGain is small, causing the per-unit gain to round to zero and SABLE to be lost from the accumulator, violating INVARIANT that all SABLE issued by SableRewarder must be accounted for in F_SABLE or returned, causing scoped impact: SABLE tokens are permanently stranded in SableStakingV2 with no staker able to claim them? Local proof idea: unit test with live totalSableLPStaked value and _SABLEGain = 1, assert F_SABLE increases by at least 1 or the dust is tracked.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SABLEToken.permit() nonce management] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER observe that _nonces[owner]++ is incremented inline in the keccak256 call under PRECONDITIONS where Solidity 0.6.11 evaluates abi.encode arguments left-to-right, causing the nonce to be incremented before or after the digest is computed depending on evaluation order, violating INVARIANT that the nonce used in the digest must match _nonces[owner] before the call and be incremented exactly once after, causing scoped impact: nonce desync allows permit replay or invalidation of legitimate permits? Local proof idea: unit test calling permit() and asserting nonces(owner) == 1 after first call and the digest used the pre-increment nonce value 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SableStakingV2 + SABLEToken.transfer()] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER observe that SableStakingV2.unstake() calls sableToken.transfer(msg.sender, SABLEGain) under PRECONDITIONS where msg.sender is a contract that reenters SABLEToken.transfer() or SableStakingV2 functions, causing state inconsistency between SABLE balance and stakes accounting, violating INVARIANT that SABLE transfers must not enable reentrant extraction of additional rewards, causing scoped impact: attacker's receiver contract extracts extra SABLE by reentering unstake() during the SABLE transfer? Local proof idea: unit test with malicious receiver reentering unstake(0) during sableToken.transfer callback, assert total SABLE received equals exactly one SABLEGain.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: CommunityIssuance.issueSABLE() linear model] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER observe that issuance = latestRewardPerSec.mul(timeSinceLastIssue) with no decay or cap under PRECONDITIONS where the contract is dormant for a long period (e.g., 1 year) and then triggered, causing a single issueSABLE() call to issue latestRewardPerSec * 31536000 SABLE in one transaction, violating INVARIANT that SABLE issuance must be bounded by the actual SABLE balance in CommunityIssuance (5409915403529186761416 live), causing scoped impact: a single large issuance event allows the first depositor to claim all remaining SABLE while later depositors receive nothing? Local proof idea: unit test advancing time by 1 year, calling issueSABLE(), assert issuance does not exceed CommunityIssuance SABLE balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SABLEToken.transferFrom() allowance check ordering] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER call transferFrom(sender, recipient, amount) under PRECONDITIONS where the allowance check _allowances[sender][msg.sender].sub(amount) happens after _transfer(), causing the transfer to succeed before the allowance is verified in edge cases with SafeMath, violating INVARIANT that allowance must be verified before any token movement occurs, causing scoped impact: attacker transfers SABLE without sufficient allowance? Local proof idea: unit test calling transferFrom with amount > allowance, assert it always reverts before any balance change.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SableStakingV2.stake() LP transferFrom timing] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER observe that in stake(), the stakes[] and totalSableLPStaked are updated BEFORE sableLPToken.transferFrom() is called under PRECONDITIONS where sableLPToken is a standard ERC20 that reverts on insufficient balance, causing the state to be updated but the LP transfer to fail, violating INVARIANT that stake accounting must only increase when LP tokens are successfully received, causing scoped impact: if transferFrom reverts, the entire transaction reverts (SafeMath), but if LP token has non-reverting transfer, attacker gets stake credit without depositing LP? Local proof idea: unit test with a mock LP token that returns false on transferFrom, assert stakes[attacker] is not increased.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SABLEToken.domainSeparator() chain fork] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER exploit the cached domain separator under PRECONDITIONS where the BNB chain forks and the new fork has the same chainId (56) as the original, causing domainSeparator() to return _CACHED_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR (since _chainID() == _CACHED_CHAIN_ID), making permits signed on the original chain valid on the fork, violating INVARIANT that permits must be chain-specific and not replayable across forks, causing scoped impact: attacker replays victim's SABLE permit on a BNB fork to drain allowance and extract tokens? Local proof idea: differential test asserting that a permit signed pre-fork is rejected post-fork when chainId is unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SableStakingV2 F_SABLE + SABLEToken.transfer() to staking] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER directly transfer SABLE tokens to SableStakingV2 (bypassing _requireValidRecipient since staking is blocked for non-rewarder) under PRECONDITIONS where the attacker is sableRewarderAddress and calls transfer(sableStakingAddress, amount), causing SableStakingV2's SABLE balance to increase without a corresponding F_SABLE update, violating INVARIANT that SableStakingV2 SABLE balance must equal the sum of all pending SABLEGain across all stakers, causing scoped impact: phantom SABLE in staking contract that no staker can claim, or first unstaker extracts more than their share? Local proof idea: unit test transferring SABLE directly to staking as sableRewarder, assert sum of all getPendingSABLEGain equals staking SABLE balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SableStakingV2.unstake() _sableLPAmount > currentStake] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER call unstake(uint256_max) under PRECONDITIONS where currentStake is nonzero, causing SableLPToWithdraw = LiquityMath._min(uint256_max, currentStake) = currentStake, then stakes[msg.sender] = 0 and totalSableLPStaked decreases correctly, violating INVARIANT that unstake must not allow withdrawal of more LP than staked, causing scoped impact: attacker extracts more LP tokens than they deposited? Local proof idea: unit test calling unstake(type(uint256).max), assert LP tokens returned equal exactly currentStake and not more.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: CommunityIssuance.sendSABLE() + SABLEToken._requireValidRecipient()] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER observe that sendSABLE(account, amount) calls sableToken.transfer(account, amount) where account could be the SABLEToken contract itself under PRECONDITIONS where StabilityPool passes address(sableToken) as the recipient, causing _requireValidRecipient to revert (recipient == address(this)), violating INVARIANT that SABLE reward distribution must never be blocked by recipient validation, causing scoped impact: SABLE rewards permanently locked if StabilityPool routes to token contract address? Local proof idea: unit test calling sendSABLE(address(sableToken), 1e18) from StabilityPool, assert it reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SableStakingV2.stake() + unstake() with zero totalSableLPStaked transition] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER be the sole staker and call unstake(currentStake) to reduce totalSableLPStaked to zero under PRECONDITIONS where F_SABLE is about to be increased by increaseF_SABLE(), causing the SABLE gain to be lost (SABLEGainPerSABLEStaked = 0 when totalSableLPStaked == 0), then restake to capture future gains without the lost period, violating INVARIANT that all issued SABLE must be distributed to stakers or returned to protocol, causing scoped impact: SABLE tokens issued during zero-stake window are permanently stranded in SableStakingV2? Local proof idea: unit test: sole staker unstakes, increaseF_SABLE called, staker restakes, assert F_SABLE did not increase and SABLE is unaccounted.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/SABLEToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Fund extraction or protocol value drain: any bug that lets a user gain, mint, borrow, redeem, withdraw, claim, or extract more funds than intended from the protocol, including minting value from nothing, flashloan-assisted extraction, oracle manipulation extraction, broken accounting, unauthorized withdrawals, over-redemption, underpayment, insolvency creation, or any other path that increases attacker-controlled funds at protocol or user expense'] [Function: SABLEToken.increaseAllowance() + transferFrom()] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER call increaseAllowance(spender, MAX_UINT - currentAllowance) under PRECONDITIONS where currentAllowance is already nonzero, causing _allowances[owner][spender] to reach MAX_UINT via SafeMath add, then call transferFrom repeatedly to drain the owner's entire SABLE balance, violating INVARIANT that allowance must reflect the owner's explicit intent and not be inflated beyond what was approved, causing scoped impact: attacker with any existing allowance inflates it to MAX_UINT and drains victim's SABLE? Local proof idea: unit test where victim calls increaseAllowance(attacker, large_amount), assert attacker can only transfer up to the explicitly approved total.
